title: salix eleagnos
intro: Salix eleagnos, also known as the silver willow or grey willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly the bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix eleagnos include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, making it useful for treating various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in the production of willow bark extracts and salicylic acid-based medications.
